name: Riaza and Hontanares Hermitage Loop
name_original: Riofrío de Riaza y Ermita de Hontanares — circular desde Riaza
description: This moderate 10.4-mile touring cycling route from Riaza explores oak forests, the Riaza River, and the historic Ermita de Hontanares.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Riaza offers diverse landscapes in the foothills of Spain's Sierra de Ayllón. The region features deep river gorges carved into limestone within the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, alongside rugged mountain terrain and extensive beech forests. Varied elevations provide options from gentle routes to more challenging ascents, connecting natural features with traditional villages. Best touring cycling routes around Riaza The most popular touring cycling route is Riaza and Hontanares Hermitage Loop, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around Riaza, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Madriguera, El Muyo y Serracín desde Estebanvela, a moderate 14.7 miles (23.6 km) path. This route connects several villages, providing a scenic and cultural experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Riofrío Reservoir – Plaza Mayor de Riaza loop from Riaza, a 16.4 miles (26.5 km) trail leading through natural landscapes and the town's central square, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Riaza is defined by deep river gorges, mountain foothills, and beech forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Riaza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 4 reviews. More than 130 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Riaza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Riaza?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes in the Riaza area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes explore the diverse landscapes from deep river gorges to mountain foothills and extensive beech forests.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Riaza?
A: The terrain around Riaza is quite varied. You'll find routes traversing deep river gorges within the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, rugged mountain terrain in the Sierra de Ayllón, and picturesque beech forests like the Hayedo de Pedrosa. Elevations range from gentle paths to more challenging ascents, connecting natural features with traditional villages.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Riaza?
A: Yes, Riaza offers a few easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and smoother paths, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without significant challenge.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Riaza?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Riaza are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111775792/riaza-and-hontanares-hermitage-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaza and Hontanares Hermitage Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that explores the area around Riaza.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Riaza's touring cycling routes offer access to numerous natural and cultural highlights. You can explore the dramatic gorges of the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, cycle through the vibrant Hayedo de Pedrosa Beech Forest, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6232525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking panoramic views, nearby peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6527980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tres Provincias&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2176894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Lobo&lt;/a&gt; offer challenging ascents and spectacular vistas.

Q: Can I incorporate cultural exploration into my touring cycling trip?
A: Absolutely. The region around Riaza is known for its unique 'Yellow, Red, and Black Villages,' distinguished by their traditional architecture and local building materials. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111796446/madriguera-el-muyo-y-serracin-desde-estebanvela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madriguera, El Muyo y Serracín desde Estebanvela&lt;/a&gt; connect several of these picturesque villages, offering a rich cultural experience alongside scenic cycling.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Riaza?
A: Riaza is enjoyable for touring cycling across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn are particularly beautiful, with pleasant temperatures and stunning natural colors—lush greens in spring and vibrant reds in the beech forests during autumn. The region's diverse landscapes offer unique beauty throughout the year.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the town of Riaza itself?
A: Yes, several routes either start, end, or pass through the charming town of Riaza. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Reservoir – Plaza Mayor de Riaza loop from Riaza&lt;/a&gt; takes you through natural landscapes and directly into the town's central square, allowing you to experience its traditional architecture and atmosphere.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Riaza?
A: The touring cycling routes in Riaza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the gorges and forests, and the opportunity to visit charming traditional villages.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists, Riaza offers a good selection of difficult routes with significant elevation gain. These routes often venture deeper into the Sierra de Ayllón, providing demanding ascents and rewarding descents. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Riaza – El Muyo loop from Riaza&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a substantial distance and elevation.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural scenery with historical sites?
A: Many routes in the Riaza area naturally blend stunning natural scenery with historical and cultural points of interest. You can cycle through protected natural parks and also encounter traditional villages with unique architecture, such as those found on the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31490321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla – Plaza Mayor de Riaza loop from Riaza&lt;/a&gt;, which connects a village with the historic Plaza Mayor of Riaza.
tips: In the old middle of the town of Riaza is this beautiful round square that is not paved but made of dirt. Beautiful square with the church as a central part.
From the Peña del Moro it is worth stopping for a few seconds to be able to observe the magnificent views of the town of Riofrío de Riaza with the Sierra de Ayllón rising in the background.

The hermitage was built in 1606 with two large rooms; one the church itself and the other the old hostelry. Inside, the main altarpiece stands out, presided over by the image of Our Lady of Hontanares. The hermitage occupies the place where the church of the village of Hontanares previously existed, now disappeared.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Riofrío de Riaza y Ermita de Hontanares circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route usually takes around 1 hour and 23 minutes to complete, though your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how long you stop at points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is considered 100% cyclable and features a mix of dirt tracks and forest paths. While some sections are easy, you should expect some uphill climbs, particularly after crossing the Riaza River and after descending into the Manadillas stream valley. The 'Camino de la Ermita de Hontanares' portion is rated as medium difficulty.

faqs.question: Where does the Riofrío de Riaza y Ermita de Hontanares route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Riaza. You can typically find parking in or around the town center, which is a convenient starting point for this circular trail. The Plaza Mayor de Riaza is a central landmark in town.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable spots. The route offers stunning panoramic views of the Sierra de Ayllón, including peaks like Pico Lobo. A key highlight is the Hontanares Hermitage, a 17th-century religious building nestled in an oak forest. Near the hermitage, the Mirador de Piedras Llanas provides views of the Central and Iberian Systems and the distinctive 'red and black villages'. You'll also get a View of Riofrío de Riaza and follow the course of the Riaza River.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While the route is moderately challenging with some uphill sections, it is generally considered 100% cyclable. Families with some cycling experience and beginners who are comfortable with moderate climbs and mixed terrain may enjoy it. However, it's not a flat, easy ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: This route can be enjoyed year-round. However, for the most pleasant experience, spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder conditions, though the trail remains cyclable.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Riofrío de Riaza y Ermita de Hontanares cycling route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, especially if kept on a leash. However, always be mindful of local regulations, especially when passing through villages or near livestock. Ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the Riofrío de Riaza y Ermita de Hontanares circular route. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around the Hontanares Hermitage is well-equipped. You'll find recreational facilities there, including picnic areas with tables and stone barbecues, as well as a restaurant. Riaza itself also offers various cafes, pubs, and restaurants.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Natural de la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, the Ruta del Sureste, and the Camino de Riofrío de Riaza, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see while cycling?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through significant oak forests, with trees often covered in moss and lichens. The route also offers stunning panoramic views of the Sierra de Ayllón and follows the serene course of the Riaza River, providing a diverse natural landscape.
